claims.claim: Under CC-CV charging mode, the maximum temperature difference between internal and external battery temperatures at 0.5 C overcharge is 55°C, and at 7 C overcharge is 29.5°C.
claims.quote: Under the CC-CV charging mode, the temperature differences between the internal and external maximum temperatures at 0.5 C and 7 C overcharges are 55 ◦C and 29.5 ◦C, respectively.

claims.claim: Under CC charging mode, the maximum temperature difference between internal and external battery temperatures at 0.5 C overcharge reaches 75.2°C and at 7 C overcharge reaches 66.1°C.
claims.quote: Under the CC charging mode, the maximum temperature differences between the inside and outside of the battery at 0.5 C and 7 C overcharges reach 75.2 ◦C, respectively... C and 66.1

claims.claim: During CC overcharging at 5 C and 7 C, the battery pressure relief valve opens, with the maximum internal temperature reaching 115°C.
claims.quote: CC high-rate overcharging (5 C, 7 C) results in the activation of the battery's pressure relief valve, with the maximum internal temperature reaching 115 ◦C.

claims.claim: During CC overcharging, the pressure relief valve opens at 988 s for 5 C overcharge and at 686 s for 7 C overcharge, resulting in a sharp drop in internal temperature.
claims.quote: when the battery is overcharged at 5 C and 7 C for 988 s and 686 s respectively, the pressure relief valve opens, resulting in a sharp drop in internal temperature.

claims.claim: The voltage mutation threshold during CC overcharging increases with increasing overcharge rate, ranging from 5.0037 V at 0.5 C to 5.2610 V at 7 C.
claims.quote: during the 0.5 C overcharge period, the voltage mutated at 5.0037 V; during the 1 C overcharge period, the voltage abruptly changed at 5.0409 V; during the 3 C overcharge period, the voltage abruptly changed at 5.1587 V; during the 5 C overcharge period, the voltage abruptly changed at 5.2548 V; and during the 7 C overcharge period, the voltage abruptly changed at 5.2610 V.

claims.claim: When both parameters Poutlier and Pr>δ of the TR data are less than 0.05, the case is classified as sudden TR; otherwise, it is categorized as gradual TR.
claims.quote: when both parameters Poutlier and Pr>δ of the TR data are less than 0.05, the case is classified as sudden TR; otherwise, it is categorized as gradual TR.

claims.claim: The combined MOSE-AC and MMSE-LOF method achieves a 100% detection rate for gradual TR cases and an 86% detection rate for sudden TR cases.
claims.quote: when the two algorithms are combined, they achieve a 100 % detection rate for gradual TR cases and an 86 % detection rate for sudden TR cases

claims.claim: For gradual TR cases, the MOSE-AC algorithm triggered early warnings an average of 16,800 frames in advance, corresponding to approximately 6 days prior to TR.
claims.quote: For gradual TR cases, MOSE-AC was able to trigger early warnings an average of 16,800 frames in advance, corresponding to approximately 6 days prior to the TR.

claims.claim: For sudden TR cases, the MOSE-AC algorithm provided warnings an average of 9,400 frames ahead of actual TR onset, equivalent to roughly 3 days.
claims.quote: In sudden TR cases, the algorithm provided warnings an average of 9400 frames ahead of the actual TR onset, equivalent to roughly 3 days.

claims.claim: In gradual TR pre-failure data, the voltage extreme difference fluctuated between 0.1 V and 0.2 V, whereas in sudden TR pre-failure data, the voltage extreme difference fluctuated between 0 V and 0.02 V.
claims.quote: the voltage extreme difference of the E-bikes all had a fluctuation phenomenon of 0.1 V to 0.2 V... the voltage extreme difference of the E-bikes all fluctuates between 0 V and 0.02 V.

claims.claim: For 50 Ah LFP cells, immediate 1C discharge under forced convection at 105% SOC limits capacity degradation to 2.8% ± 0.3%, which is below the 3% industry failure criterion.
claims.quote: immediate 1C discharge at the reversible point limited the capacity degradation to 2.8 % ± 0.3 % (n = 3), which is significantly below the industry failure threshold of 3 %

claims.claim: Exceeding the 105% SOC reversible overcharge threshold results in irreversible capacity loss exceeding 7.5% ± 0.5%, accompanied by widespread rupture of the negative electrode SEI film.
claims.quote: samples charged beyond this limit experience a sharp capacity loss exceeding 7.5 %, with electrochemical analysis revealing widespread rupture of the negative electrode's solid electrolyte interphase (SEI) film

claims.claim: Early thermal anomalies with a temperature rise rate exceeding 0.03°C/s were detected 1693 ± 45 s before thermal runaway onset, which is 1193 s earlier than voltage collapse detection.
claims.quote: The early thermal warning signal was detected 1693 ± 45 s before thermal runaway onset... Infrared thermal monitoring offers a crucial early warning advantage by detecting abnormal local temperature rise rates (>0.03 C/s) approximately 1693 s before thermal runaway—significantly earlier than voltage-based detection by 1193 s.

claims.claim: During normal charging (SOC < 100%), the battery surface exhibits lateral thermal stratification with a maximum temperature difference of 18°C, aligned with tab geometry due to current concentration effects.
claims.quote: below 100 % SOC, lateral thermal stratification (ΔT_max = 18 C) aligned with tab geometry... During normal charging, the battery surface exhibits a stable horizontal thermal stratification, characterized by significantly higher temperatures near the tab (lug) region compared to areas farther away. This pattern is attributed to the skin effect of current flow

claims.claim: At 105% SOC, the thermal field transitions from lateral stratification to a concentric pattern with a central hotspot, occurring 38 ± 5 s before mechanical deformation becomes visible to the naked eye.
claims.quote: at 105 % SOC, a transition to concentric thermal fields with central hotspots, emerging 38 ± 5 s prior to mechanical deformation... This reversal in thermal stratification occurs about 38 ± 5 s before the bulge becomes visible to the naked eye

claims.claim: Once battery SOC exceeds 120%, the temperature rise rate increases sharply to 0.62°C/s, and a 10% SOC increment drives surface temperature from 49°C to 86.1°C, compared to an average rate of 0.013°C/s between 90% and 120% SOC.
claims.quote: Between 90 % and 120 % SOC, the temperature rises slowly at an average rate of 0.013 C/s... However, once the battery exceeds 120 % SOC, the temperature rise rate increases sharply to 0.62 C/s, and a mere 10 % SOC increment drives the surface temperature from 49 C to 86.1 C

claims.claim: Thermal energy peaked at 37.9 kJ approximately 80 minutes into overcharging, followed by a 15% decline marking the transition from constant-current to constant-voltage charging, where cooling is identified as most effective.
claims.quote: Thermal energy peaked at 37.9 kJ approximately 80 min into overcharging, followed by a 15 % decline—marking the critical transition from constant-current to constant-voltage, where cooling is most effective

claims.claim: If the voltage increase rate exceeds 0.9 mV/s and no inflection point is observed, the battery can be definitively judged to have irreversibly transitioned into the thermal runaway pathway.
claims.quote: if the voltage increase rate exceeds 0.9 mV/s and no inflection point is observed, the system can be definitively judged to have irreversibly transitioned into the thermal runaway pathway

claims.claim: The multi-parameter early warning strategy combining temperature rise rate, spatial thermal uniformity, and voltage second derivative achieves a false positive rate of 4.7% and enhances warning lead time by 32% ± 5% compared to single voltage-based detection.
claims.quote: The multi-parameter warning strategy enhanced the lead time by 32 % ± 5 % compared to voltage-based detection alone... Multi-parameter warning false positive rate: 4.7 %

claims.claim: The reversible overcharge threshold of 105% SOC and associated thermal evolution laws are specific to fresh 50 Ah prismatic aluminum-shell LFP batteries at 25°C; universality for different capacities, packaging forms, states of health, or extreme temperatures requires further investigation.
claims.quote: the reversible overcharge threshold (105 % SOC) and the associated thermal evolution laws identified in this study are specific to fresh 50 Ah prismatic aluminum-shell LFP batteries under a standard temperature of 25 C. The universality of this threshold for cells with different capacities, packaging forms (e.g., pouch, cylindrical), states of health (SOH), or under extreme temperature conditions requires further investigation

claims.claim: During the irreversible overcharge trajectory, the temperature rise rate in the triangular region near the pressure relief valve increased significantly after the C state (105% SOC), while under reversible overcharge the negative electrode region exhibited a 28% enhancement in cooling due to endothermic lithium-ion reinsertion.
claims.quote: In the irreversible overcharge trajectory, the temperature rise rate in the triangular region near the pressure relief valve (measurement points 10–12) increased significantly after the C state, highlighting the intensive heat release associated with electrolyte decomposition. In contrast, under reversible overcharge, the negative electrode region (point 7) exhibited a 28 % enhancement in cooling, attributed to the endothermic effect of lithium-ion reinsertion following active discharge intervention.

claims.claim: Gas sensor false alarm rates can reach as high as 30% under complex operational environments due to cross-sensitivity to environmental conditions.
claims.quote: the cross-sensitivity of gas sensors to environmental conditions results in false alarm rates as high as 30 % under complex operational environments
